steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage moves from 1889 (when the Eiffel Tower's design was criticized) to the present (when the design is commonplace). The blank needs a transition word that signals the present moment, showing how things have changed over time.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — On the contrary,: signals logical opposition, not a shift in time. ✗
  • Choice B — In conclusion,: wraps up an argument rather than marking a move to the present. ✗
  • Choice C — Furthermore,: adds supplementary information without indicating any temporal shift. ✗
  • Choice D — Nowadays,: anchors the sentence in the present and captures the contrast between the tower's past controversy and its current status as widely replicated architecture. ✓
D is correct.
